title: Auganthus cinerascens
taxonomy: Auganthus cinerascens is classified in the following way: it belongs to the family Apiaceae, a group of flowering plants commonly known as the carrot or parsley family, and its classification is as follows - Auganthus cinerascens is a genus in the tribe Oenantheae. The genus Auganthus is often included in the genus Seseli. However, the classification can be complex and subject to change as more research emerges. The classification may also vary based on different taxonomic systems used. Further research is needed to confirm the exact classification of Auganthus cinerascens.

intro: Auganthus cinerascens, commonly known as False Pimpernel, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Apiaceae. The health benefits of Auganthus cinerascens include its potential to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its use in traditional medicine for treating various ailments. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Auganthus cinerascens include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to aid digestion and alleviate respiratory issues.
